summary: The portfolio climbed a full tier off last week's at-risk: 3 healthy / 3 needs-attention / 0 at-risk, nothing stalled. The dashboard-writer clobber that defined last week is closed across the board, and three long-standing HIGHs cleared in one week (YaC brand rename, HBH commit-per-patch, YaB .finances leak gone from history). Handler drift went clean. What holds it at needs-attention is the host side: 13 queue items are file-complete but stranded on a Windows .git/index.lock clear plus release.ps1, the pill-vs-changelog class recurred one cycle after BR closed it, and FUSE truncation escalated from files to .git metadata.

topActions.label: Add a preship gate to every release.ps1 that refuses a version-pill bump with no matching changelog footer entry
Clear the stale .git/index.lock portfolio-wide and run the 13 stranded committed-ready release.ps1 ships
Commit Scheduler now: it is one command from healthy and the git-guard.ps1 safety subsystem is uncommitted
Promote Scheduler's git-guard.ps1 to a shared portfolio helper against FUSE .git corruption
Cut Apothecary v1.0 (98%, 8 clean cycles) and drop it plus YaC to weekly review
Re-verify the .finances leak scrub from an authed machine to drop YaB to healthy
git rm --cached the five tracked scripts/*.ps1.bak files and add a *.bak gitignore rule
Log the 3D-render profiler baseline before gameplay drives the crowd at scale
Fix handler dependency shorthand and refresh the handler-audit skill path table
Wire new Yes& Agents project into the bar-raise loop and dashboard
